{"path": "python_guide.txt", "content": "================================================================================
                    COMBINED PYTHON GUIDE
         Best Practices & Testing Frameworks
================================================================================

TABLE OF CONTENTS
-----------------
1. Python Best Practices
   1.1. Code Style & PEP 8
   1.2. Project Structure & Organization
   1.3. Virtual Environments & Dependency Management
   1.4. Type Hints & Documentation
   1.5. Error Handling
   1.6. Performance Considerations
   1.7. Security Best Practices
2. Python Testing Frameworks
   2.1. Overview of Testing Frameworks
   2.2. unittest (Standard Library)
   2.3. pytest
   2.4. nose2
   2.5. doctest
   2.6. Hypothesis (Property-Based Testing)
   2.7. Framework Comparison Table
3. Testing Best Practices
4. Putting It All Together: A Sample Project

================================================================================
SECTION 1: PYTHON BEST PRACTICES
================================================================================

1.1 CODE STYLE & PEP 8
-----------------------
PEP 8 is the official style guide for Python code. Key guidelines include:

  - Indentation: Use 4 spaces per indentation level (no tabs).
  - Line Length: Maximum 79 characters per line (72 for docstrings/comments).
  - Blank Lines: Use two blank lines around top-level functions/classes; one
    blank line around method definitions inside a class.
  - Imports: Place imports at the top of the file, grouped in this order:
      1. Standard library imports
      2. Third-party imports
      3. Local application imports
     Separate each group with a blank line.
  - Naming Conventions:
      - Classes: CapWords (CamelCase) convention (e.g., MyClass)
      - Functions, variables, attributes: lowercase_with_underscores (snake_case)
      - Constants: ALL_CAPS
      - Non-public methods/attributes: _leading_underscore
      - Name mangling (for subclasses): __double_leading_underscore
  - Whitespace: Avoid extraneous whitespace (e.g., inside parentheses, before
    commas, etc.).
  - Comments: Use complete sentences. Inline comments should be separated by
    at least two spaces from the code.

Tools to enforce PEP 8:
  - flake8: Linter that checks PEP 8 compliance
  - black: Opinionated code formatter (auto-formats to PEP 8)
  - isort: Sorts imports automatically
  - pylint: Comprehensive linter with style checking

1.2 PROJECT STRUCTURE & ORGANIZATION
--------------------------------------
Recommended project layout for a typical Python package:

    my_project/
    ├── README.md
    ├── LICENSE
    ├── pyproject.toml          # Modern project configuration
    ├── setup.py                # (or setup.cfg) for package metadata
    ├── requirements.txt        # Pinned dependencies for production
    ├── requirements-dev.txt    # Development dependencies
    ├── .gitignore
    ├── src/
    │   └── my_package/
    │       ├── __init__.py
    │       ├── module1.py
    │       ├── module2.py
    │       └── subpackage/
    │           ├── __init__.py
    │           └── ...
    ├── tests/
    │   ├── __init__.py
    │   ├── test_module1.py
    │   ├── test_module2.py
    │   └── conftest.py         # pytest shared fixtures
    ├── docs/
    │   └── ...
    └── scripts/
        └── ...

Key principles:
  - Keep modules focused (Single Responsibility Principle).
  - Use packages (directories with __init__.py) to organize related modules.
  - Separate source code from tests.
  - Use a src/ layout to avoid import confusion during testing.

1.3 VIRTUAL ENVIRONMENTS & DEPENDENCY MANAGEMENT
--------------------------------------------------
Always use virtual environments to isolate project dependencies.

  - venv (built-in):
      python -m venv venv
      source venv/bin/activate   # Linux/Mac
      venv\Scripts\activate      # Windows

  - pipenv: Combines Pipfile with virtual environment management.
  - poetry: Modern dependency management with pyproject.toml.
  - conda: For data science projects with non-Python dependencies.

    ├── src/
    │   └── my_package/
    │       ├── __init__.py
    │       ├── module1.py
    │       ├── module2.py
    │       └── subpackage/
    │           ├── __init__.py
    │           └── ...
    ├── tests/
    │   ├── __init__.py
    │   ├── test_module1.py
    │   ├── test_module2.py
    │   └── conftest.py         # pytest shared fixtures
    ├── docs/
    │   └── ...
    └── scripts/
        └── ...

Key principles:
  - Keep modules focused (Single Responsibility Principle).
  - Use packages (directories with __init__.py) to organize related modules.
  - Separate source code from tests.
  - Use a src/ layout to avoid import confusion during testing.

1.3 VIRTUAL ENVIRONMENTS & DEPENDENCY MANAGEMENT
--------------------------------------------------
Always use virtual environments to isolate project dependencies.

  - venv (built-in):
      python -m venv venv
      source venv/bin/activate   # Linux/Mac
      venv\Scripts\activate      # Windows

  - pipenv: Combines Pipfile with virtual environment management.
  - poetry: Modern dependency management with pyproject.toml.
  - conda: For data science projects with non-Python dependencies.

Dependency files best practices:
